channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Fermeture des comptes PlayStation inactifs après 3 ans : pourquoi la panique autour de cette clause de Sony est injustifiée
item.description: Une clause des conditions d'utilisation de PlayStation affole les réseaux : un compte PSN inactif peut être fermé, jeux numériques compris. Sauf que la règle n'a rien de nouveau.

item.title: Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI
item.description: "Group project, but make it 1776." That's how a new commercial for Google Workspace opens. And things only get cringier from there. The clip imagines what it would be like if the founding fathers turned to Google's collaboration tools and Gemini to help them draft the Declaration of Independence. Ben Franklin texts Thomas Jefferson to […]

item.title: « C’est un big deal » : le patron de Fiat nous parle de ses futures voitures électriques à 10 000 et 15 000 €
item.description: C'est officiel, Fiat est de retour et cible un segment bien particulier : celui des petites voitures. Après la Topolino sans permis, la marque compte bien lancer une citadine électrique à 15 000 € en parallèle d'un quadricycle à 10 000 €. Quelles stratégies ? Quelles contraintes ? Nous en avons discuté avec Olivier François, CEO de Fiat, et Guillaume Clerc, son responsable produit. ...

item.title: The Sourdough Sidekick automates the boring bit of baking
item.description: Baking sourdough bread is inherently old-fashioned, relying on natural fermentation and wild yeast instead of the simple, predictable commercial stuff. So it might sound anathema to bring a gadget into the mix. The trick to the Sourdough Sidekick - backed and branded by King Arthur flour - is that it promises to automate the boring […]

item.title: I've been on the hunt for the perfect tablet to travel with — here are the 5 models at the top of my wishlist
item.description: Portable, bump-proof, and with long battery life to keep you entertained during journeys and down-time.

item.title: I tested the Ninja AutoBarista Pro, and its smart Grind iQ feature demystifies grind settings so you don’t have to master coffee science for a decent latte
item.description: Grind iQ Technology in the Ninja AutoBarista Pro eliminates the need for guesswork when it comes to the perfect grind size for your particular beans.

item.title: Midjourney pushes to expose studios own AI practices in copyright fight
item.description: Midjourney seeks studio AI records as it fights a landmark copyright suit from Disney, Universal, and Warner Bros.

item.title: How Keurig saved — and ruined — your coffee
item.description: Before Keurig, the coffee in your office was almost certainly terrible. Old, burned, made by someone who would rather poorly eyeball than properly measure. Just altogether gross. After Keurig? You could make your own coffee, a cup at a time, exactly when you needed it. The single-cup brewer was an elegant solution to an extremely […]

item.title: What is Bending Spoons? The little-known AOL and Vimeo owner that’s now public
item.description: Bending Spoons remains largely unknown, even as its portfolio of products has served more than a billion people.

item.title: Nokia's new retro-styled feature phones have AI buttons for some reason — and users are dismissing the odd feature as 'useless' and 'dumb'
item.description: HMD has unveiled four new feature phones, but there are question marks over the inclusion of an AI button.

item.title: These popular smartphones are in their last year of software support
item.description: It's good to know how long your phone will get updates before you purchase.

item.title: Cette nouvelle trottinette électrique robuste et endurante est déjà soldé chez Boulanger
item.description: La trottinette électrique NAVEE V40i Pro II est un bon choix pour vos trajets urbains quotidiens. À l'occasion des soldes chez Boulanger, ce nouveau modèle s'affiche déjà en promotion à 329 euros au lieu de 349 euros.

item.title: Un événement cosmique il y a 2,5 millions d’années aurait bousculé le nuage d’Oort et lancé des comètes vers nous !
item.description: La mission Gaia permet de faire de l'archéologie galactique en remontant dans le temps en ce qui concerne les positions et trajectoires passées de bien des étoiles. Elle nous renseigne indirectement sur l'histoire passée du Système solaire et notamment celle des comètes à longues périodes.

item.title: Vizio accidentally made the best dumb TV on the market
item.description: When I first started testing Vizio's 65-inch Mini LED Quantum TV, I thought the big story was that Vizio was back and that it had a quantum-dot TV for under $398 - the cheapest on the market. Vizio's been pretty quiet since it was acquired by Walmart in 2024, so putting out a TV with […]

item.title: I've tested the most advanced mirrorless cameras ever made, but I'd still choose this seven-year-old Canon DSLR any day
item.description: The Canon EOS 1DX Mark III might be getting a little long in the tooth by digital standards, but it offers some unique advantages that the recent EOS R1 flagship simply can't match

item.title: T-Mobile is giving away the Apple iPhone 17 for free — heres how to claim
item.description: How to get the Apple iPhone for free. Score the Apple iPhone 17 or iPhone 17 Pro for free with T-Mobile.

item.title: Almost 90 new unicorns have been minted so far this year — here they are
item.description: With AI igniting an investor frenzy, more startups are achieving unicorn status every month.
